channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, next 30 days.

item.title: Textile Exchange x Atmos: Fashion Regenerated 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Textile Exchange (with Atmos)

Location: Open call; work to be promoted globally through Textile Exchange and Atmos and exhibited at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference.

Award: Overall winner: a paid collaborative commission worth USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, published through Atmos and Textile Exchange and shown at the 2026 conference. Two runners-up: work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, plus a USD 2,000 cash prize.

Duration: One-off commission/prize.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

Fashion Regenerated is an open call from Textile Exchange and Atmos. Winners are selected by a panel of expert judges and, for the first time, a public vote. The shortlist is promoted through Textile Exchange and Atmos, amplifying inspirational stories to an engaged audience of thousands. The overall winner receives a paid collaborative commission to the value of USD 10,000 with Textile Exchange to develop a new body of work documenting the evidence of regeneration, working closely with the team to identify the right story and access; the winning work is published through Atmos, Textile Exchange, and shown to an audience of more than 1,300 changemakers at the 2026 Textile Exchange conference. Two runners-up have their work promoted by Textile Exchange and displayed at the conference exhibition, and there is also a USD 2,000 cash prize. Check the call site for full eligibility and submission details. Deadline: 20 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Feminist Gaming Fellowship: Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Feminist Gaming Project

Location: Remote across the Netherlands, Türkiye, Germany, and the UK

Award: EUR 1,000 honorarium per fellow (paid half at the start, half on completion)

Duration: Three months, August to October 2026; minimum ~6 hours per week (~75 hours total)

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

The Feminist Gaming Project is opening applications for its first Fellowship cohort: five fellows from different creative and research fields spending three months developing 3D assets, tools, and documentation for its Open Access Asset Library (OAL), a free, open repository of inclusive 3D content created by, with, and for intersectionally feminist and diverse communities. Where mainstream marketplaces default to hyper-athletic, idealized, or hyper-sexualized characters, the OAL offers average bodies, real postures, everyday objects, and the kinds of lives most games leave out (pregnancy and postpartum, care work, ageing, disability, domestic labor). This first cohort is dedicated to 3D content. Supported by the Creative Industries Fund NL and working across the Netherlands, Türkiye, Germany, and the UK, the project challenges exclusionary norms in how games are made. The fellowship offers a EUR 1,000 honorarium per fellow over three months (half at start, half on completion); mentorship from the core team and advisors in feminist design, game studies, 3D production, and digital culture; a peer community across NL, TR, DE, and the UK with biweekly meetings; visibility through a travelling exhibition program, publications, and public events; and authorship credited in the OAL under its license terms. Five roles are recruited (one fellow each): 3D Character Artist for inclusive base meshes; 3D Character Artist for pregnancy and postpartum assets; 3D Animator for neutral and everyday movement; 3D Modeller for domestic and care-labor environments; and Researcher/Writer for documentation and the representation framework; applicants may rank up to two. Open to 3D artists, animators, modellers, designers, researchers, and writers committed to inclusive and intersectional representation in games; prior game-industry experience is not required, and emerging creators, underrepresented practitioners, and researchers at the intersection of games, gender, and culture are especially encouraged, particularly those based in or connected to NL, TR, DE, or the UK. To apply, email feministgamingproject@gmail.com with a short statement (max ~500 words), a portfolio or work samples (a link is fine), and confirmation of availability for the three-month period; name the role(s) in the subject line or statement. The project explicitly asks applicants to write statements in their own voice rather than using AI-generated text. Indicative timeline: applications close 12 July 2026; selection and notification late July; fellowship period August to October 2026; showcase and publication November to December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Loghaven Artist Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Loghaven Artist Residency

Location: Knoxville, Tennessee, USA

Award: USD 850 weekly living stipend, travel subsidy USD 400 to 800, up to USD 200 materials shipping reimbursement, plus room and board, housing in historic log cabins, and studio space USD 25 non-refundable application fee (fee waiver available on request)

Duration: 2 to 6 weeks depending on session

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Loghaven Artist Residency offers practicing artists time, space, and resources on ninety wooded acres in Knoxville, Tennessee. Fellows stay in five rehabilitated historic log cabins with access to purpose-built studio space. Each Fellow receives an USD 850 weekly living stipend, a travel subsidy on a sliding scale from USD 400 to 800, up to USD 200 in materials shipping reimbursement, and covered room and board. Residencies run from 2 to 6 weeks across sessions scheduled throughout 2027. The program is open to artists at any career stage working in architecture, dance, interdisciplinary art, music composition, theater, visual art, and writing, including collaborative groups of up to nine people. Applicants must be at least 21, live more than 120 miles from Knoxville, and be eligible to work legally in the US; international artists and degree-seeking students are not eligible.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MSB Interpretive Panels: Public Art Commission (Creative Philadelphia)
item.description:
Organisation: Creative Philadelphia (Office of Arts, Culture and the Creative Economy)

Location: Municipal Services Building, Center City, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, United States. Philadelphia-based artists strongly encouraged; artists from outside the region considered. Multiple in-person site visits required (travel costs covered within the project budget).

Award: Total project budget of USD 250,000, which must cover: artist fees (18% design fee), design and engineering, fabrication and installation, structural footings/anchoring, travel and site visits, AR development (software, hosting, content production), insurance (1%), community engagement participation, and a service warranty keeping content compatible with new mobile OS versions for a minimum of 10 years. No application fees.

Duration: Permanent public artwork commission; two-part selection process.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Creative Philadelphia announces a call to commission a series of permanent interpretive panels with integrated augmented reality (AR) components at the Municipal Services Building in Center City Philadelphia. The commission is part of a broader initiative that includes an upcoming public sculpture of Dr. Sadie T.M. Alexander by artist Vinnie Bagwell. Inspired by Alexander's groundbreaking legacy in public service, these interpretive panels will celebrate the City of Philadelphia's municipal employees (past, present, and future) and provide deeper context for understanding Dr. Alexander's impact; they will be located in front of the building's landscaped areas, flanking the upcoming sculpture, and are intended to complement and support the overall site experience. SCOPE: an artist team will design and fabricate four (4) permanent, sculptural interpretive panels incorporating augmented reality (AR) elements to expand storytelling beyond the physical surface of the artwork. ELIGIBILITY: all interested artists are invited to apply; Philadelphia-based artists are strongly encouraged, though artists from outside the region will be considered; multiple in-person site visits are required and associated travel costs must be covered within the project budget. SELECTION: a two-part process: Part 1 submission of qualifications; Part 2 finalist proposals and interviews. Up to three (3) finalists are invited to submit short concept proposals and attend an interview; at no point should artists submit design proposals. BUDGET: USD 250,000 total (see amount for the required cost breakdown). An Artist Informational Meeting is held via Zoom on 30 June, 6:00-7:00pm. Application questions: paige.phillips@phila.gov. Apply via Submittable. Deadline 12 July 2026 (submissions close Monday 13 July 2026, 6:00 AM).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Core Echo 2027: International Open Call & 3-Week Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Grandtek Art Center

Location: On-site residency, likely in China (organizer uses WeChat, stipend in RMB, and works enter a partner enterprise's collection; exact city not stated in the call). International open call.

Award: Initial stipend of RMB 20,000 per selected artist. Round-trip travel, accommodation, and daily meals provided during the on-site residency. Dedicated co-creation studios provided (with supplementary temporary creation zones from partner enterprises). One piece from each artist's exhibited series enters the permanent collection of the partner enterprise, per a contract signed by the end of the residency.

Duration: Three-week on-site residency.

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Core Echo 2027 is an international open call from Grandtek Art Center for a three-week on-site artist residency. SUPPORT PACKAGE: each selected artist receives an initial stipend of RMB 20,000, with round-trip travel, accommodation, and daily meals provided during the on-site residency. One piece from each artist's exhibited series will enter the permanent collection of the partner enterprise, with terms specified in a contract signed by the end of the on-site residency period. Dedicated co-creation studios are provided, and selected partner enterprises offer supplementary temporary creation zones; spatial requirements requested by artists will be met to the fullest extent possible. Weekly artist salons are held during the residency for peer exchange and exhibition planning. The call is announced via the organizer's WeChat channel; check the source for full details and the application form. Deadline: 15 July 2026, 15:59 UTC.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Wassaic Project 2027 Residency Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: Wassaic Project

Location: Wassaic, New York, USA

Award: Studio space and a private bedroom in a shared house provided; the organization covers approximately USD 5,000 per person through donor and grant support. Residency fee on a sliding scale of USD 0 to 900 per session. USD 25 application fee (waivers available for financial hardship)

Duration: Sessions range from roughly 2 to 4 weeks within the Winter (Jan-April 2027) or Summer (June-Sept 2027) programs

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

The Wassaic Project runs a single 2027 Artist Residency open call covering both the Winter Residency (January to April 2027) and the Summer Residency (June to September 2027) in Wassaic, New York. Residents receive a private studio (about 100 square feet in winter, 200 to 300 square feet in summer), a private bedroom in a shared house with common spaces and kitchen, and access to a wood shop in both seasons and a print shop in summer. Programming includes open studios, group studio visits, and individual artist talks. The residency operates on a sliding scale fee of USD 0 to 900 per session, with the organization covering roughly USD 5,000 per person through donor and grant support. The open call is open to individual artists, collaborative teams, groups of two or more, and applicants through the Family Residency program. This is the residency program, distinct from the separate Summer Exhibition open call.

Open call details

item.title: Artist Residency Autumn 2026 (Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies, Palermo)
item.description:
Organisation: Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies

Location: Altarello di Baida district, Palermo, Sicily, Italy. On-site 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026. Open to all nationalities, but applicants must be officially residing in a Creative Europe country (excluding Italy).

Award: Total allowance of EUR 1,860 (before tax) per artist, plus a travel budget of EUR 400 per artist (for distances under 4,000 km) with a EUR 400 green-mobility top-up, plus a production budget. Also provided: on-site accommodation in a fully furnished independent house (private bedroom; shared kitchen, living room, two bathrooms; outdoor studio space 100m from the working site), access to recycled/salvaged materials through the Centre's partnerships, a bicycle per resident, and a final public event.

Duration: 62-day residency, 12 October - 12 December 2026, structured in five phases (orientation, observation/fieldwork, proposal and ecological feasibility, creation, and public restitution).

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

The Centre for Multispecies Relations and Future Ecologies invites applications from two artists for a 62-day residency (12 October - 12 December 2026) at its site in the Altarello di Baida district of Palermo, Sicily. The residency is structured around a 1,500 sqm garden at the threshold between the city's urban expansion and the historic rural landscape of the surrounding hills; the site, degraded through previous use (soil compaction and significant biodiversity loss), functions as both studio and subject, and artists participate in an ongoing process of ecological transformation, producing interventions that emerge from extended observation and fieldwork. Applications are welcome across all mediums and disciplines (installation, sound, performance, video, food art, participatory practice, and beyond); research-based and interdisciplinary practices are especially encouraged. The Centre works at the intersection of art, science, and (agro)ecological practices, with a focus on multispecies coexistence and climate change in the Mediterranean, and has been based in Palermo since 2021. PROGRAMME: five phases (orientation with small trips and gatherings; observation with independent fieldwork and consultations with artists, curators, scientists, and anthropologists; proposal and ecological feasibility developed with mentors; creation; and public restitution with an open event, a workshop, and a public discussion). Each artist develops an independent site-specific intervention; the two residents share the site, living space, and process, with exchange encouraged but not prescribed. Artist duos and artists who want to collaborate are encouraged to apply (you can apply as an individual for one position, as a duo for both, or as two artists collaborating for two positions). Mentorship by co-founders/artistic directors Fabio Aranzulla and Luca Cinquemani (weekly site visits, studio discussions, work-in-progress presentations); logistics managed by Francesca Farris. WHO: artists whose work engages with ecology, multispecies relations, or the more-than-human, who can move between observation and making and are interested in dialogue with scientists, land workers, and local communities. Priority is given to artists from working-class and less-privileged backgrounds, those who self-identify as part of marginalised communities, and candidates reflecting diversity in gender, age, geographical background, and ability; green travel is prioritised as a condition of participation where feasible. Applicants should be open to living and working in a neighbourhood on the margins of Palermo's periphery. Funded by Culture Moves Europe (Creative Europe programme of the European Union). APPLICATION: first phase via the online form (opened 10 June); shortlisted artists contacted before the end of July; second phase is video calls with shortlisted candidates; applications accepted in English and Italian. Contact: contact.cmrfe@gmail.com. Deadline 5 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Shanty Projects 2027: Call for Performances + Art Actions
item.description:
Organisation: Art Shanty Projects

Location: Bde Uman (Lake Harriet), Minneapolis, Minnesota, United States. On-ice program plus several mandatory in-person meetings in Minneapolis; in-person participation required.

Award: Sliding-scale stipend up to USD 1,000 (request an amount that fits the project: roughly USD 1,000 for full-day/most-ambitious activities, USD 500 for half-day, USD 250 for short or one-off/solo/low-production activities). Plus direct production support, discounted materials and resource sharing, a shared warming shanty, a hot meal, snacks, hot beverages, hand/toe warmers, one parking space per project, on-site medic, and ASL interpreter/audio describer for the public. No application fees.

Duration: On-ice program every Saturday and Sunday, 10am-4pm, 16 January to 7 February 2027, with production meetings and mandatory check-ins from September 2026 onward.

Deadline: 09 Jul 2026

Art Shanty Projects seeks winter enthusiasts and creative adventurers to create performances and art actions for its 2027 Program on Bde Uman (Lake Harriet) in Minneapolis. It invites music-makers, poets, builders, scientists, dancers, puppeteers, writers, dreamers, activists, outdoors-people, conversationalists, craftspeople, storytellers, and more to propose new and innovative ways to engage artistically with the public in the social context of a village on a frozen lake (or winter 'beach', as climate change may necessitate). ASP has an expansive notion of what constitutes a performance or art action, having welcomed dance, creative sporting events, happenings, carnival games, music, roving dance, temporary sculpture, puppet shows, artful haircuts, advice booths, climate actions, and more. NOTE: this call is for PERFORMANCES + ART ACTIONS only (a separate call exists for shanty artists). Since 2004, Art Shanty Projects has created joyful winter art experiences inspired by pop-up ice fishing villages, using the frozen lake as a public platform for a temporary creative community with tens of thousands of annual visitors. ASP is deeply committed to social, racial, gender, and economic justice and to accessibility (embracing the spirit of the ADA). ELIGIBILITY: everyone is eligible (artists at all career stages); applicants need not have experience with everything asked, but should show eagerness to engage, develop new skills, and stay open to new perspectives. Projects that involve selling anything or the exchange of legal currency will not be accepted. COMPENSATION: sliding-scale stipend up to USD 1,000 plus extensive production and logistical support. PROGRAM: on-ice every Saturday and Sunday, 10am-4pm, 16 January to 7 February 2027; selected artists notified mid-August 2026, with mandatory in-person meetings (at least one team member) starting 10 September 2026. Apply via the Google form (preferred) or the no-Google-account alternative. Contact Erin Lavelle, Artistic Director: program@artshantyprojects.org. Deadline for proposal submissions: 9 July 2026, 11:59pm CST.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Windgate Artist-in-Residence Fall 2026 (Arkansas Tech University)
item.description:
Organisation: Arkansas Tech University (supported by the Windgate Charitable Foundation)

Location: Russellville, Arkansas, United States (Russellville campus, in the Arkansas River Valley, about one hour from Little Rock). In-person, full-time residency.

Award: USD 30,000 stipend (minus fringes) plus USD 18,000 for supplies. Housing provided (a 2-bed, 2-bath campus apartment) and a large, climate-controlled, dedicated off-campus studio space.

Duration: Five-month residency over the Fall academic term 2026.

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

The Artist-in-Residence (AIR) program at Arkansas Tech University is an immersive experience within an art and educational community, intended to advance the careers of practicing artists, present residents as role models for the ATU community, and strengthen public awareness of the significance of making art and its effect on the surrounding community. The residency takes place over a five-month period, giving the artist time and space to focus on the creative process. For Fall 2026, the program will select an artist to create a public artwork that enhances the ATU campus; both two-dimensional and three-dimensional proposals will be considered. The program is supported by a generous grant from the Windgate Charitable Foundation. The Department of Art at ATU offers programs in Fine Art, Graphic Design, Game and Interactive Media Design, and Art Education, with a facility in Norman Hall featuring a professional gallery, computer lab, lecture hall, and studios for painting, drawing, printmaking, sculpture, ceramics, and graphic design. RESIDENCY DETAILS & EXPECTATIONS: residents must be artists with significant exhibition records, strong communication skills, sustained studio practice, and a desire to engage with the larger campus community; involvement may include guest lecturing, public talks, mentoring students, open studios, and/or collaborating with faculty and students. The resident sometimes teaches one course in 2D Design, 3D Design, or Introduction to Drawing during their stay when enrollment allows. Artists are expected to commit as a full-time resident for the Fall academic term 2026. BENEFITS: USD 30,000 stipend (minus fringes), USD 18,000 for supplies, a 2-bed/2-bath campus apartment, and a large climate-controlled off-campus studio. Deadline 3 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artivism & Amplifiers Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Human Impacts Institute

Location: Global; pairs young climate leaders from the Global North and Global South. Open to speakers of English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese.

Award: USD 1,000 stipend upon completion. Supported by expert workshops and creative mentors.

Duration: Six-month program.

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

The Artivism & Amplifiers (A&A) Fellowship pairs young climate leaders from the Global North and Global South to co-create multimedia campaigns, exhibits, and installations for the Creative Climate Awards 2027. Over six months, fellows build skills in intercultural collaboration, climate storytelling, and community engagement, supported by expert workshops and creative mentors. The program is open to speakers of English, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. Fellows receive a USD 1,000 stipend upon completion. The organizers strongly encourage applications from people most impacted by the climate crisis, including those from the global majority, Indigenous, LGBTQIA+, immigrant, and diaspora communities, and people with disabilities. Applications close Wednesday 1 July 2026 at 9am GMT+2.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AY-ROOP / Le Milieu: Reuse - (Re)making for Circus Residency 2026 (Rennes Métropole, France)
item.description:
Organisation: AY-ROOP

Location: Le Milieu, Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole, France. Applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe country other than France and the French overseas territories (DROM-COM).

Award: Funded through Culture Moves Europe: EUR 900 mobility allowance per person plus flat-rate travel coverage; supplements for green mobility, visa fees, dependent children under 18, OCT/OR residents, and disability/accessibility needs (including possible full coverage of a sign-language interpreter). On-site single-room accommodation, lunches at Le Milieu canteen (Tuesday to Friday), shared kitchen, and technical support provided. No application fee.

Duration: 21-day residency, 28 November to 18 December 2026.

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

AY-ROOP is launching an open call to select three circus artists and/or circus set designers/builders for a 21-day Culture Moves Europe research residency at Le Milieu (Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole) from 28 November to 18 December 2026. The residency is a research laboratory on reuse in contemporary circus: how everyday, locally available materials (paper/cardboard, plastics, lightweight metal, textiles, reclaimed wood, bicycle parts, etc.) can be used to create scenographic elements, sets, props and small apparatuses for juggling, acrobatics and balancing. The core principle is that objects are recreated locally from resources available in each territory rather than transported. AY-ROOP provides an equipped construction workshop, a rehearsal stage and circus tent, a guide to local reuse-sector contacts, and a full residency environment (accommodation, shared kitchen, working facilities); two mentors (a reuse specialist and a performing-arts professional) support the artists. PROFILES: circus artists interested in construction/scenography/DIY making, and builders/set designers working in circus; individual or group applications (max 3 people per group); emerging or established, any background. Accessible to artists with disabilities and accessibility needs. Working language English. No aerial apparatus or rigging: all work is ground-based. ELIGIBILITY: aged 18+ (no upper limit); legally resident in a Creative Europe country other than France and DROM-COM; available for the full residency. APPLY with a single PDF (1-page bio/CV, 5-page portfolio or video links, 1-page statement of intent, proof of residence). DEADLINE: 3 July 2026. Results announced 13 July 2026. Check the linked call PDF for full details.

Open call details

item.description:

Location: East and Central Asia (Kazakhstan, Japan, South Korea, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Uzbekistan, Mongolia)

Unruly Mirrors is an open call for artists based in Kazakhstan, Japan, South Korea, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Uzbekistan, or Mongolia. The project examines beauty practices in East and Central Asia as spaces of norm reproduction, intimacy, and feminist resistance. Financial support and full eligibility criteria were not publicly confirmed at time of entry (the website was unreachable). Deadline: 15 July 2026. Apply or inquire at https://unrulymirrors.com. item.title: Pioneer Works: 2027 Open Call – Visual Arts and Music Residency (Brooklyn, NY)
item.description:
Organisation: Pioneer Works

Location: Pioneer Works, Red Hook, Brooklyn, New York, USA

Award: Visual Arts: USD 5,000 one-time award (USD 2,500 honorarium + USD 500/month stipend for 5 months). Music: USD 3,000 one-time award (USD 2,500 honorarium + USD 500/month stipend for 1 month). Free studio space and facilities access. No housing or travel provided. No application fee.

Duration: Visual Arts: 5 months. Music: 1 month. Cohort begins after jury decisions in late September 2026.

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Pioneer Works in Red Hook, Brooklyn invites applications from emerging visual artists and musicians for its 2027 residency cohort. 12 Visual Arts residents (5-month residency) and 12 Music residents (1-month residency) are selected each year through a two-phase jury process. WHAT IS PROVIDED: free studio space; access to facilities including risograph printer, darkroom, ceramics studio, large-format printer, 3D printers, thermobinder, and laser cutter; biweekly studio visits with curators, gallerists, critics, and artists; free access to all public programmes at Pioneer Works, including Second Sundays (monthly open studios and performances). FINANCIAL AWARD: USD 5,000 for Visual Arts residents; USD 3,000 for Music residents (each comprising a USD 2,500 honorarium plus USD 500/month for the residency duration); applicants' choice on how to spend it. Pioneer Works does not provide housing, travel, or visa sponsorship. ELIGIBILITY: open locally, nationally, and internationally; collectives may apply (resources shared); current students and programme alumni are not eligible. REVIEW PROCESS: phase one is internal scoring on technical ability, uniqueness of vision, and future potential (nontraditional career paths given special consideration); highest scorers advance to an external jury of art professionals and alumni who assess diversity of demographics and media, genuine studio need, and fit with Pioneer Works' public-facing culture. Jury convenes September 2026; all applicants notified by late September. Supported by Dalio Philanthropies, the Andy Warhol Foundation, NYC Department of Cultural Affairs, New York State Council on the Arts, and the National Endowment for the Arts. APPLY: via Pioneer Works website by 13 July 2026. Questions: residency@pioneerworks.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 (Documentary Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Women Photograph (supported by Leica USA)

Location: Remote (international)

Award: Three grants of USD 10,000 each (USD 30,000 total). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (new or in-progress photography projects).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 awards three grants of USD 10,000 each to support documentary photography projects. ELIGIBILITY: open to women, nonbinary, transgender, and intersex documentary photographers of any nationality; no membership required; no restrictions on camera equipment or career stage. Funding covers hard costs of reporting, photographers' creative fees, and any other expenses that support the production of new or in-progress still photography projects. Not intended for books, exhibitions, or workshops. Grantees retain full work ownership. One submission per applicant; applications in English (Google Translate acceptable); any use of generative AI must be disclosed. Applications opened 20 May 2026; deadline 30 June 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Apply at https://app.picter.com/contests/bad7ea97-7b0d-4a9c-9e85-38e73529d62b/start.

Open call details

item.title: Nakanojo Biennale 2027: International Artist Open Call (Gunma Prefecture, Japan)
item.description:
Organisation: Nakanojo Biennale Executive Committee / Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture

Location: Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture, Japan

Award: Production grant provided (amount not specified publicly). No exhibition fee, no residency fee. Insurance subsidies available. Artists responsible for materials, food, transportation, and installation/removal costs. No application fee.

Duration: Exhibition: 18 September – 24 October 2027 (37 days). Production period: April–August 2027. Residency accommodation available from April 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Nakanojo Biennale 2027 (11th edition) is an international open call for artists of all genres — sculpture, painting, photography, video art, installation, performance, and more — to exhibit in Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. Theme: 'Echoing Green', inspired by William Blake's poem, exploring cycles of life, regeneration, and connection between past and future generations. Open internationally to individual artists and artist groups at all career stages; new participants are encouraged. A production grant is provided (amount not publicly specified); no exhibition or residency fees are charged. Residency accommodation available from April 2027. Staff support provided for venue selection and installation guidance. First-time participants in Japan must attend an on-site venue inspection; an online orientation on 5 September 2026 is required for all accepted artists. Deadline: 12 July 2026 at 23:59 JST (online). Apply via Google Form at https://forms.gle/SS8DaaGA6uwUKogj7. Full details at https://nakanojo-biennale.com/2027-open-call/english.html. Contact: office@nakanojo-biennale.com.

Open call details

item.title: Samstag International Visual Arts Scholarships - Class of 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Samstag Museum of Art, University of South Australia

Location: Australia (applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents); 12-month placement at an international institution of the scholar's choice.

Award: AUD $75,000 tax-exempt stipend; institutional fees for one academic year of study (where applicable); return airfares; and travel and health insurance. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months overseas at an international learning institution.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

The Samstag International Visual Arts Scholarships are annual scholarships enabling Australian artists to develop their artistic capacities through a dedicated year of practice-based learning at an international institution of their choice. AMOUNT: AUD $75,000 tax-exempt stipend; institutional fees for one academic year (where applicable); return airfares; and travel and health insurance. ELIGIBILITY: Australian citizens or permanent residents aged 18 or over who are either (a) currently enrolled in a practice-led visual arts program at an Australian institution of higher education, having successfully completed at least two years of an undergraduate or postgraduate degree; or (b) graduates of a practice-led visual arts undergraduate or postgraduate qualification at an Australian institution of higher education (graduates of more than five years' standing are now eligible following 2023 guideline updates). PROCESS: applicants must create an account in the online portal, download the Samstag Microsoft PowerPoint template, read the Class of 2027 Guidelines, and complete and submit the online application form. Accounts can be created before the deadline and applications saved for later submission. Prior applicants (Classes of 2024, 2025, or 2026) can reuse the same account. Deadline: 30 June 2026, midnight Australian Central Standard Time (ACST). Apply at https://samstag.adelaide.edu.au/scholarship/apply/.

Open call details

item.title: ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026: Open Call (Vienna, Austria)
item.description:
Organisation: Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography

Location: Academy of Fine Arts, Vienna, Austria

Award: EUR 250 cash award per selected winner + EUR 150 travel allowance. Grand Prix winner receives an additional EUR 250, a solo exhibition, and a free hotel stay. Submission fee: EUR 20 per project.

Duration: Exhibition at Academy of Fine Arts Vienna; announcement August 2026, public reveal September 2026.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026, organised by Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography, selects 20 projects for exhibition at the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna. Theme: 'Anthropocene: Hybrid Realities' – exploring the tension between human ecological dominance and environmental fragility. Each selected winner receives: exhibition in a 1,300+ m² venue, publication in the official festival catalogue (2 copies), EUR 250 cash award, EUR 150 travel allowance, festival pass, and free registration for Photodays Rovinj 2027. Grand Prix winner additionally receives a solo exhibition, an extra EUR 250, a free hotel stay, and a catalogue cover feature. ELIGIBILITY: open to any individual aged 16 or older, at any career stage, internationally; submissions must include at least one analog photographic process; works must have been taken within the last 5 years (2021-2026); no AI-generated or AI-enhanced work (unless deliberately thematic). Files must be JPEG, max 5 MB, minimum 2000px on longest side, 300 dpi. Jury selects winners by late July 2026. Deadline: 30 June 2026. NOTE: EUR 20 submission fee per project applies. Apply at https://www.rotlicht-festival.at/open-call-submission/.

item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and Arab League Educational, Cultural and Scientific Organization (ALECSO)

Location: International (Francophone zone and Arab world)

Award: EUR 10,000 (funded equally by OIF and ALECSO). No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

The Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize (19th edition, 2026) is awarded jointly by the Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and ALECSO to recognise excellence in literary and humanities/social sciences translation between French and Arabic in either direction. Cash award: EUR 10,000. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual translators, universities and higher education institutions, research centres, national associations and unions, and publishing houses from the Arab world and Francophone space. Works must be literary or humanities/social sciences texts translated between French and Arabic (either direction), published within 3 years prior to the application year, and must be a first translation of the original. Self-published or photocopied works excluded. One work maximum per candidate. Applications submitted exclusively electronically; required materials: CV, presentation of translation work, copies of both translated and original texts. Deadline: 30 June 2026. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Location: UK (both director and producer must be UK residents)

Award: Up to £25,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Short films 5–40 minutes; up to 13 projects funded per year

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

BFI Doc Society's Made of Truth fund supports early-career UK filmmakers making non-fiction live-action or animated short films of 5–40 minutes. Up to 13 projects are funded per annual round. AMOUNT: up to £25,000 per project. ELIGIBILITY: early-career directors and producers who are UK residents; teams must have at least two people (a producer and a director); directors cannot have received a commercial distribution deal for a non-fiction feature in the last five years; a reasonable proportion of early-career crew is required. Applications must meet at least three of six priorities: equity/diversity/inclusion, audience impact, talent development, creative risk, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. item.title: Sandberg Instituut: Call for Proposals, Temporary Department 2027-2029 (Head of Department, The Archive)
item.description:
Organisation: Sandberg Instituut

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands; applicants must be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate for the duration of the programme.

Award: Freelance position as Head of the Temporary Department, approx. two days per week over two academic years (September 2027 to July 2029), plus allocated hours for curriculum development (October 2026 to July 2027). Approximate daily rate EUR 553.60. The department is also supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a dedicated department coordinator plus administrative support, and institutional infrastructure and guidance. A modest fee is provided for the second-round interview stage. No application fee.

Duration: Two-year Temporary Department, September 2027 to July 2029 (curriculum development from October 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

Sandberg Instituut invites artists, designers, researchers, makers and educators to propose a Temporary Department, taking on the role of Head of Department for the period September 2027 to July 2029. THEME, THE ARCHIVE: for this edition the institute seeks proposals that engage with the archive as a contemporary artistic, social, political and pedagogical framework. Increasingly recognised as a site of power, selection, memory, erasure and imagination, the archive is approached here not as a static collection but as a living, contested and generative process to be activated, reinterpreted and transformed through contemporary practice, and as a material and methodological field to work with, against and through. Possible starting points include: archives as sites of resistance, repair and restitution; embodied, oral and performative archives; collective and community-based archives; digital, algorithmic and AI-driven processes; counter-archives and practices of refusal; speculative archives and future-oriented forms of memory; creative practices that activate, reinterpret or transform existing archives; and the politics of preservation, access and knowledge production. Proposals should articulate a compelling approach to the archive plus a clear, connected pedagogical vision for how collective research unfolds over two years while supporting individual trajectories; partnerships with external organisations (museums, social archives, artist-led initiatives, community organisations and research institutions) are encouraged. FORMAT: Temporary Departments are full-time two-year master's studies bringing a cohort of students and tutors together around a specific field of inquiry; successful completion leads to a Master in Fine Arts and Design accredited by the NVAO. POSITION: the freelance Head of Department role is equivalent to approx. two days per week over the two academic years plus curriculum-development hours (October 2026 to July 2027), at an approximate daily rate of EUR 553.60, supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a department coordinator with administrative support, and institutional infrastructure. WHO CAN APPLY: applicants should have teaching/education experience; be able to develop and coordinate a two-year MA curriculum rooted in their own research and practice; have strong communication and leadership skills; have cross-disciplinary collaborative experience; be committed to an inclusive, supportive learning environment responsive to diverse access needs; be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate; hold an established independent professional practice registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KVK); and, if applying from outside the EU, hold a valid Dutch residence and work permit for the appointment. SELECTION (two rounds): first-round submissions to td@sandberg.nl by 17:00 on Sunday 5 July 2026 (a max. 500-word department proposal, a max. 200-word networks and collaborations statement, and a max. 2-page CV); first-round notification 15 July 2026; interviews with shortlisted candidates 16 September 2026. Questions: td@sandberg.nl.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Circolo del Design: Earthrise 26 Residency for Young Designers 2026 (Turin)
item.description:
Organisation: Circolo del Design

Location: Turin, Italy; in-person residency.

Award: Designer's fee of EUR 1,500 (VAT included); travel and accommodation covered for both phases of the residency; a public transport pass; up to EUR 3,000 (VAT included) for project production; a dedicated 30 sqm workspace within Circolo del Design; plus networking, mentorship from interdisciplinary experts, and technical, logistical and organisational support. No application fee.

Duration: Residency 5 October to 3 December 2026

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

Earthrise 26 (Design for a Living Planet), the annual cultural initiative by Circolo del Design, will unfold around clay and earthenware, approached not as functional objects but as narrative, spatial and speculative matter. One of the projects featured in the exhibition will be developed through the Earthrise 26 Residency (5 October to 3 December 2026), a unique opportunity for an under-35 international designer to create and experiment within an interdisciplinary context. Under the mentorship of Designregio Kortrijk and BASE Milano among others, the selected resident will collaborate with local designers, artisans and interdisciplinary experts in Turin to produce an original body of work exhibited as part of the Earthrise 26 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: under 35; a demonstrated international practice through research, collaborations, cultural exchange or mobility within Europe; well-structured project planning with clear goals; thematic relevance aligned with the objectives of Earthrise 2026; and an experimental and/or innovative approach. WHAT IS OFFERED: a designer's fee of EUR 1,500 (VAT included), travel and accommodation for both phases, a public transport pass, up to EUR 3,000 (VAT included) for project production, a dedicated 30 sqm workspace, and mentorship and technical/logistical/organisational support. Organised by Circolo del Design (circolodeldesign.it; contact programma@circolodeldesign.it). Deadline: 3 July 2026; results announced 20 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: AIR Taipei: International Artist Residency 2027 (Taipei Artist Village & Treasure Hill)
item.description:
Organisation: Taipei Culture Foundation (AIR Taipei, operating Taipei Artist Village and Treasure Hill Artist Village)

Location: Taipei, Taiwan; on-site residency.

Award: Funding varies by track. INTERNATIONAL RESIDENCY (open to international artists): free accommodation and studio space plus administrative and project support; no cash artist grant. PUPPETRY RESIDENCY (Treasure Hill x Puppetry Art Center, open to international artists): NT$20,000 (around USD 620) subsidy plus accommodation and studio. OVERSEAS RESIDENCY VISIT (Taiwanese artists only): NT$100,000 (around USD 3,100) creative grant plus economy-class roundtrip airfare. No application fee.

Duration: Approx. 6 weeks to 3 months depending on category (International Residency roughly 8 to 12 weeks), in 2027

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

AIR Taipei is the international artist-in-residence program run by the Taipei Culture Foundation across the Taipei Artist Village and Treasure Hill Artist Village. The 2027 open call welcomes artists, curators, researchers, performers, writers, cultural practitioners and interdisciplinary creators working in visual art, performance, music, architecture, literature, curatorial practice, cultural studies and interdisciplinary arts. TRACKS AND ELIGIBILITY: international applicants can apply to the International Residency (free accommodation and studio space plus administrative and project support, roughly 8 to 12 weeks, no cash artist grant) and to the Treasure Hill x Puppetry Art Center Project Residency (NT$20,000, around USD 620, subsidy plus accommodation and studio). The Overseas Residency Visit, which carries a NT$100,000 (around USD 3,100) creative grant and economy-class roundtrip airfare, is open to Taiwanese artists only and does not apply to international applicants. Residency length runs approximately 6 weeks to 3 months depending on the category and host institution, taking place in 2027. International applicants must submit a resume and residency plan in English or Chinese. APPLY at https://air-artistvillage.org.tw/ before 11:59:59 (Taipei time, late morning) on Tuesday 30 June 2026. Questions: air@artistvillage.org.

item.title: SVĚTOVA 1: As We Grow V (Educational & Research Program) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: SVĚTOVA 1

Location: Prague, Czech Republic; participants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program.

Award: Scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200). Free 14-day educational programme; work exhibited at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks with production and PR support. NO accommodation provided (funding restrictions). No application fee.

Duration: 14 days, 7 to 20 September 2026

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

SVĚTOVA 1 invites early-career artists and cultural organizers to apply for the fifth edition of its educational and research programme As We Grow. This year the course is led by artist and mythopoet András Cséfalvay, Associate Professor at the Academy of Fine Arts in Bratislava. The 14-day project (7 to 20 September 2026) culminates in a group exhibition. WHAT YOU GET: a free 14-day educational experience; a scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200); a rich working environment with on-site interaction with the leading artist, scholars and other artists; active involvement in the As We Grow research and event programming; organizational and professional support; and exhibition of your work at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks, with production and PR support before, during and after the show. SVĚTOVA 1 actively encourages queer and BIPoC people who have migrated to or taken refuge in Prague to apply. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program, have essential spoken English, and commit to the whole 14 days after acceptance. Note: accommodation cannot be offered due to funding restrictions. APPLY via the online Notion form with a portfolio of 3 works, a CV (max 2 pages), and either a 2-3 minute video or a 250-word motivation letter (may include images) outlining how your lived experience and interest align with the course. Deadline: 30 June 2026 at midnight; results announced by 15 July 2026. Questions: tomas@svetova1.cz.

item.title: Platform: Residency Program 2027 (Loosey-Goosey), Vaasa, Finland
item.description:
Organisation: Platform

Location: Vaasa, Finland; artist must spend at least 80 percent of the residency in Vaasa.

Award: Fully funded. Working grant of EUR 42/day (proportional to days spent in Vaasa), a material budget up to EUR 1,000 (reimbursed against original receipts for project-related purchases), and travel covered (one return trip, cheapest option). Accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: 4 to 12 weeks during 2027 (applicant specifies preferred months)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Platform is an art organization in Vaasa, a relatively peripheral Finnish town, running an art space and a residency. The 2027 theme, Loosey-Goosey, invites working artists to explore the limits of their expression and question professionalism: what happens to our art when we define and limit it to conform to society's lines, and who decides what makes a professional artist. Platform seeks artists who work with the topic of professionalism, questioning it or rejecting result-driven societal pressures and norms. The residency is open to artists of any medium, including but not limited to installation, performance, sound art, textiles, sculpture, video, community art, and painting. SUPPORT: a fully funded residency with a private apartment (bedroom and kitchen) within walking distance of the dedicated studio and project space; a working grant of EUR 42/day (proportional to days spent in Vaasa); a material budget up to EUR 1,000 reimbursed against receipts; and one return trip (cheapest option) covered. A contact person helps the artist settle in, source materials and make connections. Platform holds basic tools and electronics (projectors, Zoom recorder, office printer, sound system, sewing machine, etc.). DURATION: 4 to 12 weeks during 2027; artists who can stay up to 12 weeks are preferred, though 4-8 week requests are considered, and duos may apply. EXPECTATIONS: give a public talk, performance, workshop or presentation at the start; communicate in English, Swedish or Finnish; spend at least 80 percent of the residency in Vaasa; and submit project documentation plus a short report for the website. Platform does not run a gallery, so no end-of-residency exhibition is offered, but it will help arrange a final presentation, studio visit or performance if wanted. APPLY: via the online form only, with a single PDF (max 10 pages, 5 MB) named FIRSTNAME_LASTNAME containing (1) an informal one-page project plan and motivation letter stating preferred 2027 period, (2) descriptions and images of 5 previous projects or artworks (direct video links for videos), and (3) a CV of max two pages including name, date of birth and current location. Open call: 8 June to 30 June 2026, 23:59 EEST. All applicants notified by the end of September 2026; the board makes the selection and is not obliged to justify it.

Open call details

item.title: ARCAthens: Athens Residency Fellowships (Fall 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: ARCAthens

Location: Athens, Greece (in-person residency). Open to applicants not residing in Greece

Award: Fully funded: USD 3,000 Fellowship Prize, USD 200/week stipend toward food and local transport, basic coach round-trip airfare reimbursed, private bedroom and bathroom with shared kitchen, and studio access for Visual Art Fellows. No application fee.

Duration: 3 months, 10 September to 30 November 2026

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

The Athens Residency is the flagship program of ARCAthens, a not-for-profit founded in 2017, offering two fully-funded Fellowships (Visual Art and Curatorial) that enable artists, curators, and scholars to live and work in Athens, Greece, and respond to the city's ongoing cultural renaissance. The residency is a structured, research-driven program built around scheduled engagements with artists, curators, institutions, and collections, including arranged studio and private collection visits, guided institutional access, and select community events. Distinct from studio-isolated models, it emphasizes engagement and exchange, balancing focused visits with time for independent exploration. ELIGIBILITY: ARCAthens welcomes applicants from all countries and nationalities representing a broad range of creative thought and practice; the program is open to applicants not residing in Greece. PRACTICAL: 3-month duration; USD 3,000 Fellowship Prize; USD 200 per week stipend toward food and local transportation; a basic coach round-trip ticket is provided (Fellows purchase following ARCAthens approval of itinerary and cost, reimbursed within five business days; in cases of financial hardship ARCAthens may arrange travel directly). Fellows are responsible for all other expenses, including food, local transportation, and any production, installation, or shipping costs. Visas are not necessary for US nationals for the duration of the program; others are responsible for procuring visas as applicable. Facilities include a private bedroom and bathroom with shared kitchen; Visual Art Fellows have studio access. Two Fellows are in residence at one time. Working language is English. Selection is by a 3-person independent committee of arts professionals. No fee, open call. TIMELINE: application deadline 30 June 2026 (closes 11:00 p.m. ET); notification of Fellowships mid July; residency 10 September to 30 November 2026.

Open call details

item.title: Seoul Museum of Art: Artistic Director, 14th Seoul Mediacity Biennale
item.description:
Organisation: Seoul Museum of Art (SeMA)

Location: Seoul, South Korea (extended stays in Seoul required)

Award: Compensation approximately KRW 60 million (individual) or KRW 80 million (team). The Biennale project budget is approximately KRW 2.5 billion (based on the 13th edition). No application fee.

Duration: Part-time (flexible to full-time depending on schedule); position runs through December 2027

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

The Seoul Museum of Art (SeMA) is seeking an Artistic Director (individual or team) for the 14th Seoul Mediacity Biennale. Launched in 1996, the Biennale is the only one in Korea hosted by a museum and serves as SeMA's flagship international exhibition, a platform bringing together the rapidly changing media environment and the expanding city of Seoul. The 14th edition's exhibition is tentatively scheduled for 16 August to 14 November 2027 across SeMA venues (Seosomun Branch, parts of the Seo-Seoul Museum of Art, Paik Ground SeMA, and the Media Art Seoul Exhibition Platform). ROLE: establish the exhibition theme, develop the plan, select artists and works, oversee installation, texts, budget, related programs/publications, public relations and sponsorship, working with the Biennale team and SeMA departments. QUALIFICATIONS: demonstrated expertise in contemporary art and, within the past five years, experience as director or chief curator of an international Biennale-scale art project; ability to contribute to SeMA's networks; ability to visit and stay in Seoul for a sufficient period; and ability to work within SeMA's institutional systems. They seek a critical perspective on media and contemporary art grounded in the Biennale's identity, a future-oriented model reflecting Seoul's history and locality, and an original curatorial proposal not previously realized in Korea or internationally. COMPENSATION: about KRW 60 million (individual) / KRW 80 million (team); part-time with flexibility to go full-time, through December 2027. APPLY by email from 3 June to 15 July 2026 (midnight KST); shortlist announced around 21 August, interviews 27 August, final selection around 31 August. Inquiries: contact@mediacityseoul.kr.

Open call details

item.title: Pier-2 Art Center: Artist-in-Residence (PAIR) 2027 Open Call - Beta Port
item.description:
Organisation: Pier-2 Art Center Artist-in-Residence Program (PAIR)

Location: Kaohsiung, Taiwan

Award: A 33 sqm studio (with private bathroom) and 16.5 sqm living space; one round-trip economy airfare (international artists) or Taiwan High Speed Rail ticket (domestic); a daily living allowance of NTD 800 per artist/group (income tax and health-insurance premiums withheld); and a project-based material subsidy up to NTD 35,000. No application fee.

Duration: Residency in 2027 (full approved period; residence required throughout)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

The Pier-2 Art Center Artist-in-Residence Program (PAIR), established in 2015 and rooted in the port area of Kaohsiung, supports contemporary artistic practices that embrace experimentation and cross-disciplinary perspectives. The 2027 open call theme, 'Beta Port | An Unfinished Harbor', reflects a state of becoming, inviting artists to respond to the changing realities and imaginaries of Kaohsiung as a port city in continuous formation. Rather than merely revisiting history or collecting local materials, PAIR encourages projects that engage the city's present perceptions and lived experiences, emphasizing public engagement, local sensibility, and experimental approaches. PROVIDES: a 33 sqm studio (with private bathroom) and 16.5 sqm living space; exchange with local artistic and cultural spaces and a public presentation during the residency; access to shared facilities (multifunctional space, kitchen, dining, lecture and meeting rooms); round-trip travel (international economy airfare or domestic Taiwan HSR); a daily living allowance of NTD 800 per artist/group (taxes and premiums withheld); and a project material subsidy up to NTD 35,000. Designated staff assist with administrative coordination, resource matching and creative support, but artists manage their own daily life, material procurement, installation, and any non-English interpreting/translation. ELIGIBILITY: artists and artist groups (groups limited to two members) of any nationality and any discipline, with at least two years of artistic experience; students must be enrolled at graduate level or above; at least intermediate English is required (residency communication is primarily in Chinese and English); applicants must work and live independently and reside for the full approved period. Apply online by 5 July 2026.

Open call details

item.title: IMPAKT: Full Spectrum Curatorship Programme 2026
item.description:
Organisation: IMPAKT [Centre for Media Culture]

Location: Utrecht, Netherlands

Award: EUR 400 participation fee. Participants receive an EUR 800 stipend during the final phase to support preparation of their final project proposal.

Duration: September to December 2026; minimum 8 hours per week

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

The fourth edition of IMPAKT's Full Spectrum Curatorship Programme invites emerging and aspiring curators interested in media art, digital culture, and the intersection of technology and society to develop their own curatorial project proposal under expert guidance. Taking place in Utrecht from September to December 2026, it combines collective sessions, personal mentorship, guest contributions, and peer exchange, drawing on IMPAKT's archive and critical programming. Participants are paired with curator mentors to develop and position a curatorial proposal. The training covers the history, theory, contextualisation, organisation and execution of media-art programmes, including curatorial concept development, critical and societal contextualisation, artwork selection, budgets and funding, audience reception, and presentation formats, with a focus on time-based media and multi-channel/interactive formats. Confirmed mentors include Annet Dekker, Ine Gevers, Paulien Dresscher, Doreen A. Rios (online) and Eva Fischer (online). ELIGIBILITY: recent graduates and early- and mid-career curators; participants must attend all sessions and commit a minimum of 8 hours per week (preparation, research, independent work). All sessions are in English. COST AND SUPPORT: there is a EUR 400 participation fee, and participants receive an EUR 800 stipend in the final phase toward their project proposal. By the end, participants have a solid curatorial proposal to support future exhibitions and funding applications. Apply via the online form by 23:59 CET, 1 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ESRC Digital Good Network: Artwork Commission (Expressions of Interest)
item.description:
Organisation: ESRC Digital Good Network

Location: UK (artists based outside the UK welcome; work can be done remotely)

Award: Up to GBP 40,000 for the final commission, inclusive of VAT (capped at GBP 33,333 for artists based outside the UK due to reverse-charge VAT). Up to five shortlisted teams each receive GBP 1,000 to develop a proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Commission runs October 2026 to February 2027; first exhibition June 2027

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

The ESRC Digital Good Network is commissioning an artwork or visualisation that represents its research on the question 'what does a good digital society look like, and how do we get there?' The final piece must be simple, visually engaging and effective for multiple audiences, and will be shown in physical exhibitions (first in June 2027), online (interactive elements possible), and on a Z-fold leaflet. Two-stage process: submit an Expression of Interest (a portfolio link plus an optional one-page PDF on your approach) by 1 July 2026, 4pm UK time, to applications@digitalgood.net; up to five teams are shortlisted and paid GBP 1,000 each to develop full proposals (Aug-Sep 2026); one is commissioned in October 2026 to make the final work iteratively with two Network team members (Nov 2026-Feb 2027). Artists may visualise the Network's funded projects, its 'building blocks' of a good digital society, the values behind the Digital Good Index, or any aspect of the work. Reference points include Kate Crawford's Anatomy of AI and Calculating Empires, Dear Data by Stefanie Posavec and Giorgia Lupi, and the OECD Better Life Index. ELIGIBILITY: artists or visualisers worldwide, working alone or in teams; an optional online Q&A is held 10 June 2026. NOTE: the final output is expected NOT to use AI; if AI is used in the process, applicants must disclose how. 2D artworks have panel production and transport covered; 3D artworks must cover their own production, transport and installation.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ifa: Rave Scholarship - Cultural Heritage and Museum Transformation (2027)
item.description:
Organisation: ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen) - Alexander Rave Foundation

Location: Host institutions in Germany (applicants must be cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list)

Award: Net payment of EUR 1,500 per month; monthly family allowances for accompanying family (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance (including family); monthly public transport pass for the city of residence; up to EUR 500 for a language course. No application fee.

Duration: 3 to 6 months at a German host institution (with optional supplementary 2-12 week tandem stay in the DAC-list country if synergies arise; both stays in the same year)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Scholarship from ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen), supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, on the management of cultural heritage and the transformation of museums and exhibition centres. It enables cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list to stay with host institutions in order to deepen their professional knowledge transnationally, introduce new perspectives and build long-term cooperation through exchange. The focus is on transcultural dialogue and engaging with cultural heritage, collections and archive records, funding mutual knowledge transfer and the creation of sustainable networks, and supporting freedom in art and science, freedom of opinion and open debates. Thematic focal areas centre on transforming museums and exhibition venues in line with the ICOM definition of museums (2023): cultural participation, sustainability, non-discriminatory spaces and decolonial working methods. ELIGIBILITY: curators, restorers, mediators and culture managers from DAC-list countries. Scholarships begin after 1 February 2027.

Open call details

item.description:
Organisation: Kunstler*innen Vereinigung Tirol

Location: Kunstpavillon and Neue Galerie, Innsbruck, Austria

Award: Maximum EUR 3,500 production budget for a Kunstpavillon project or EUR 3,000 for a Neue Galerie project (covers production, display/architecture, technical rentals, materials, travel, accommodation and transport), plus a separate artist fee of up to EUR 2,000 (incl. VAT) and an additional EUR 250 fee for an accompanying artist talk. The institution additionally covers printing, translation, set-up team, marketing, PR and insurance. No application fee.

Duration: One exhibition slot within the 2027 annual programme (January 2027 - January 2028, average 9-13 weeks per exhibition)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Open call for the 2027 annual programme of Kunstler*innen Vereinigung Tirol at the Kunstpavillon (approx. 175 m2 skylight gallery) and the Neue Galerie (95 m2 vaulted space, easy to darken for projections), Innsbruck. The 2027 theme 'Thresholds' invites projects that highlight counter-models or counter-cultural dynamics to conservative, anti-modern and authoritarian backlash, exploring keywords like democracy, complexity, flash crash, anti-modern tendencies and misogyny. Up to five exhibition projects are selected. ELIGIBILITY: international call open to artists from all artistic disciplines, media and practices; concrete projects, concepts, artistic interventions and portfolios accepted. Selection is made regardless of nationality, ethnic or social background, age, disability, sexual identity or gender. APPLY (German or English) via the online platform with a completed data sheet, preferred venue, a max 500-word concept (plus a 1,200-character short description), visuals, technical info, a portfolio (films via Vimeo/YouTube/Dropbox; no WeTransfer), CVs, and a cost calculation; one PDF up to 10 MB. Postal submissions also possible (Kunstler*innen Vereinigung Tirol, Kunstpavillon, Rennweg 8a, 6020 Innsbruck, Austria). Curatorial/technical production support and a FREIRAD radio conversation accompany each exhibition. Deadline 30 June 2026; selected projects contacted by autumn 2026. Contact: office@kuveti.at.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Campo XS: CAMPO APERTO 2nd Edition (2026-2028 site-specific biennial, Genoa)
item.description:
Organisation: Campo XS (Carina Negrone, Martina Montagna; with Studio Sirotti)

Location: Campo XS, Piazza del Campo 40R, Genoa, Italy (historic Jewish ghetto)

Award: EUR 2,500 grant per selected project (covers transport, lodging, production/materials, printing, dismantling and any shipping), paid in two tranches (EUR 1,500 thirty days before opening; EUR 1,000 after dismantling) directly to the artists/curators by bank transfer, tax-free. Artists and curators based outside Italy receive an additional EUR 500 travel reimbursement. Free to apply.

Duration: Eight 60-day exhibition slots across 2026/27 (1 Oct 2026 - 25 Jul 2027) and 2027/28 (7 Oct 2027 - 30 Jul 2028)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Second edition of CAMPO APERTO, a site-specific biennial exhibition project at Campo XS in Genoa, a small (33 sqm) transparent-cube space in the historic Jewish ghetto, transformed from a former neighbourhood butcher shop. The programme selects 8 site-specific projects (60-day exhibition slots each) across two years as a laboratory for artistic and social practices engaging the surrounding neighbourhood, with exhibitions, performances and talks. The project also collaborates with the Diocesan Council for Minors and Families to bring non-typical art audiences into contact with the programme. ELIGIBILITY: national and international artists and curators, no age restrictions; projects must be led by an artist or collective in collaboration with a curator (self-curating must be justified); preference for projects pairing a national artist with an international curator (or vice versa), exploring the local urban context, with socio-ecological research, or dynamic interventions. APPLY by email (subject '2nd Edition CAMPO APERTO') to campoxs.genova@gmail.com by 30 June 2026 with an application form, project proposal, artist CV/bio and portfolio (max 30 MB), and curator CV/bio and portfolio (max 30 MB). Selected projects announced 30 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artadia Awards 2026: 21c Museum Hotels Roving Award
item.description:
Organisation: Artadia (with 21c Museum Hotels)

Location: 21c Museum Hotels partner cities, United States

Award: Three Awardees each receive a US$15,000 unrestricted award; Finalists not chosen receive an honorarium. Plus Artadia Network access and Artist Registry webpage. No application fee.

Duration: One-off roving award; two-round jury (review + virtual studio visits)

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Artadia's roving Award in collaboration with 21c Museum Hotels, providing unrestricted financial support to contemporary visual artists in cities where 21c has a presence (e.g. Louisville, Cincinnati, Bentonville, Durham, Kansas City, Lexington, Nashville, Oklahoma City, Chicago, Saint Louis). Three Awardees receive $15,000 each (use freely); Finalists receive an honorarium. Process and general eligibility match Artadia's standard rules (two-year residency in the relevant city, contemporary visual-arts practice, not a student, no prior Artadia award of $10,000+). Open call 15 June - 15 July 2026. Apply via Submittable only; check Artadia's FAQ for the specific 21c eligible cities for this cycle.

Open call details

item.title: PMRCAA Residency 2027: Process and Material (Pine Meadow Ranch)
item.description:
Organisation: Pine Meadow Ranch Center for Arts & Agriculture / The Roundhouse Foundation

Location: Pine Meadow Ranch, Sisters, Central Oregon, United States

Award: Free residency (no application or residency fee). Stipend of $200 per week to offset living and travel expenses; private studio + room in the shared Hammond House. Travel reimbursement up to $300 for qualifying residents based more than 1,000 miles one-way from the ranch (limited number; trustee-approved; paid post-residency). Stipend is conditional on completing the full residency session.

Duration: 2-week or 4-week residencies between March and mid-November 2027; cohorts of 2-4 residents share the Hammond House

Deadline: 06 Jul 2026

Residency on a working 260-acre ranch in Sisters, Central Oregon, near Bend and Redmond, for artists, ecological scientists and scholars exploring connections to nature, land conservation, historic preservation, agriculture and community building. THEME 2027: Process and Material - investigating the fundamentals of practice on a working ranch; materials as memory and meaning; cross-disciplinary work welcome including ceramics, photography, textiles, arborology and beyond. STUDIOS: 9 studio spaces adapted to different mediums (Tent Cabin, Kiln Room, Pickle Room, Old Shop, Tack Room, two Hammond House studios, Cooper's Penthouse with flatbed scanner/inkjet/GlowForge laser cutter; Studio 6000 off-site for printmaking; the Dairy Barn is unavailable in 2027). 24/7 access; private studio per resident plus shared Hammond House (private room with shared/private bathroom, kitchen, dining, laundry). Wi-Fi in main spaces and select studios. The property is NOT ADA-accessible (historic site). Residents contribute via a community-engagement element (workshop, artist talk, or Studio Tour for the public; participation in Studio Tour is required). ELIGIBILITY: US-based applicants only; emerging and established artists/scientists; no specific educational qualifications; collaborations submit one joint application. Alumni may re-apply every two years. COVID-19 vaccination required (or medical exemption discussed in advance); pets not allowed; ranch equipment and farm machinery off-limits without permission. TIMELINE: application opens 1 May 2026 (Slideroom); virtual info session 15 May; application closes 6 July 2026 at midnight PST; finalist interviews 12-14 August; references due 24 August; decisions announced September 2026. Selected by panel of external reviewers active in PMRCAA's mission areas.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: WeHo Artist Grant 2027 (City of West Hollywood)
item.description:
Organisation: City of West Hollywood, Arts Division

Location: West Hollywood, California, United States

Award: $6,000 per artist (total category pool of $30,000/year, so approximately 5 awards). Funds may be used in any capacity to fulfil the proposed project within the calendar year. No application fee.

Duration: Calendar-year project; mandatory public presentation at WeHo City Hall in November 2027 plus a 3-5 minute film summarising the project

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

Direct-to-individual artist grant from the City of West Hollywood aimed at nurturing the long-term development of an artist's practice: realising work, advancing conditions of creation, and navigating the complexities of making art and making a career. The City's goal is to keep artists in West Hollywood, attract new artists, and contribute to the city's economic and social well-being. Awardees produce a 3-5 minute film describing the project and its contribution to quality of life in WeHo, and present publicly at City Hall in November 2027. ELIGIBILITY (strict): legal address must be in the City of West Hollywood (no exceptions; proof of residency may be required); applicants must be registered on the West Hollywood Artists Registry; previous WeHo Artist Grant recipients are NOT eligible; students cannot apply in their discipline of study; cannot also be funded by another City Division or Department or co-sponsored by a Council office for the same project; only one grant category per artist per calendar year; City elected/appointed officials, employees and immediate family ineligible. PROJECTS NOT FUNDED: fundraisers, capital campaigns, murals, or religious-based programmes/events. REVIEW CRITERIA (40 points): vision and clarity of project (10), impact of funding on the applicant and WeHo community (10), portfolio (10), professional resume (10); peer-review panel forwards recommendations to the Arts and Cultural Affairs Commission. TIMELINE: deadline 3pm Wed 1 July 2026; panel reviews August 2026; ACAC approves September 2026; notifications and mandatory orientation November 2026; earliest award of funds March 2027 (subject to contracting compliance). Submit two work samples (news clips do not count) plus a clear creative process and budget.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Aerowaves Twenty27: Call for Emerging European Choreographers
item.description:
Organisation: Aerowaves (Spring Forward festival hosted in Limerick, Ireland)

Location: Festival in Limerick, Ireland (14-17 April 2027) plus partner venues across Europe; applicants must be resident in geographical Europe

Award: For presentation at Spring Forward: a fee plus expenses including travel within Europe, hotel and a per diem/hospitality for each team member. Around 100 partner performance opportunities are guaranteed each year and supported by Aerowaves. Selected applicants additionally receive a year of Aerowaves promotion (artist profile, images, video, press coverage). No application fee.

Duration: One year of Aerowaves promotion and partner-network presentations; presentation at Spring Forward 14-17 April 2027

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Aerowaves selects 20 emerging choreographers based in geographical Europe each year (the Twenty27 cohort) to have their work presented at the Spring Forward festival (14-17 April 2027 in Limerick, Ireland) and at many of Aerowaves' partners around Europe; Aerowaves promotes the selected artists for a year, creating performance opportunities with partners. EVERY APPLICANT (selected or not) has the chance to be programmed by Aerowaves network partners; around 100 performance opportunities per year are supported by Aerowaves. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be resident in geographical Europe; due to the Russian invasion of Ukraine, Aerowaves partners do NOT accept applications of works made in Russia or Belarus. ONE work per applicant per year. The work must have been produced in geographical Europe; must be 15-40 minutes; must be finished and have been presented to the public (a studio sharing counts; work-in-progress or rehearsal footage does NOT). The work should be easily included in a double or triple bill with simple technical requirements; works created for public/non-conventional spaces and outdoors are welcome. WHAT SELECTED ARTISTS GET: presentation fee at Spring Forward plus travel (within Europe), hotel and per diem/hospitality for each team member; a year of Aerowaves website promotion. DEADLINE: 15 July 2026. APPLY via the Aerowaves how-to-apply page.

Open call details

item.description:
